name: State Executive and State Legislature

sourceRefs.label: 2026_q page 34 Q33 (a resigning Minister submits the letter to the Governor, with answer key)
2026_q page 109 Q129-130 (Governor's ordinance power=Article 213; CM-appointment true statements, with answer key)
2026_q page 110 Q131 (Assertion-Reason: CM holds office at Governor's pleasure because Governor appoints the CM, with answer key)
2026_q page 29 Q28 (Articles 178/180/182/184 matched to Speaker/Chairman provisions, with answer key)
clearias.com/vajiramandravi.com - Governor's qualifications (Article 157), conditions of office (Article 158)

researchNotes: 5 real hits from a single dense cluster of pages in the 2026 question bank — State Executive is clearly well-tested. Real exam evidence repeatedly probes WHO a given action is directed at/by (resignation TO the Governor not the CM; CM appointed BY the Governor not the President; other Ministers appointed by the Governor ON THE ADVICE OF the CM) rather than plain definitions — and a precise Article-number match for the Speaker/Chairman provisions (178/180/182/184).

reading.intro: Covers the Governor (State Executive head), the Chief Minister and Council of Ministers (real executive authority), and the State Legislature's presiding officers (Speaker/Deputy Speaker of the Assembly, Chairman/Deputy Chairman of the Council). Real exam evidence shows precise traps about who appoints/receives resignations from whom, and exact Article numbers for presiding-officer provisions.

qa.question: How is the Governor appointed, what are the qualifications, and what is the normal term?
qa.answer: Appointed by the PRESIDENT (Article 155) by warrant under hand and seal — making the Governor effectively a nominee of the Central Government. Qualifications (Article 157): must be a citizen of India and at least 35 years of age (only two requirements). Normal term: 5 years, but holds office at the PRESIDENT's pleasure (Article 156) — can be removed/transferred before term's end. One person CAN be appointed Governor of two or more states simultaneously.

qa.question: What are the conditions of the Governor's office under Article 158?
qa.answer: The Governor must NOT be a member of either House of Parliament or of any State Legislature (must vacate such a seat upon appointment), and must NOT hold any other office of profit. The Governor is entitled to use of the official residence (Raj Bhavan) rent-free, and emoluments/allowances that cannot be reduced during the term.

qa.question: Real exam: To whom does a resigning member of the State Cabinet (a Minister) submit their resignation letter?
qa.answer: To the GOVERNOR — not to the Chief Minister, not to the Speaker, and not to their political party's President. (Ministers are formally appointed BY the Governor, so resignation is also submitted to the Governor, even though by convention they typically inform the CM as well.)

qa.question: Real exam: Under which Article does the Governor have ordinance-making power, and what does this power allow?
qa.answer: Article 213 — allows the Governor to promulgate Ordinances (having the force of law) when the State Legislature is not in session and the Governor is satisfied that immediate action is necessary.

qa.question: Real exam: Which statements about the appointment of a State's Chief Minister are TRUE — appointed by the President; a non-MLA may be appointed CM; other Ministers appointed by the Governor on the CM's advice?
qa.answer: TWO of the three are true: a person who is NOT currently a member of the State Legislature MAY be appointed Chief Minister (though they must then get elected to the Legislature within 6 months, per established constitutional convention); and other Ministers ARE appointed by the Governor on the advice of the Chief Minister (Article 164(1)). The FALSE part: the Chief Minister is appointed by the GOVERNOR, not the President.

qa.question: Real exam (Assertion-Reason): Why does the Chief Minister hold office 'during the pleasure of the Governor', and is this a genuine explanation?
qa.answer: Both statements are TRUE, and the Reason correctly EXPLAINS the Assertion: the Chief Minister holds office at the Governor's pleasure precisely BECAUSE the Chief Minister is appointed by the Governor (Article 164(1)) — though in practice, 'pleasure' is a formality; real continuance in office depends on retaining the confidence/majority support of the Legislative Assembly, not the Governor's personal discretion.

qa.question: What does Article 163 establish regarding the Governor's Council of Ministers?
qa.answer: There shall be a Council of Ministers headed by the Chief Minister to AID AND ADVISE the Governor in the exercise of their functions — EXCEPT in matters where the Governor is required by the Constitution to act in their own DISCRETION (a distinguishing feature from the President, who has no comparable general discretionary power).

qa.question: Real exam: Match Articles 178, 180, 182, and 184 to their correct provisions about the Assembly's and Council's presiding officers.
qa.answer: Article 178: establishes the Speaker and Deputy Speaker of the Legislative Assembly. Article 180: the POWER of the Deputy Speaker (or other person) to perform the duties of the Speaker's office. Article 182: establishes the Chairman and Deputy Chairman of the Legislative Council. Article 184: the POWER of the Deputy Chairman to perform the duties of the Chairman's office — confirmed via a real exam's match-the-following question.

qa.question: What is the key difference in discretionary power between the Governor and the President?
qa.answer: The Governor has certain situations requiring personal discretion (e.g., reserving a bill for the President's consideration, or recommending President's Rule) — a discretionary power the President generally lacks, since the President almost always acts on the Council of Ministers' aid and advice with no comparable independent discretion.

qa.question: How many Indian states currently have a bicameral legislature (a Legislative Council in addition to the Assembly)?
qa.answer: Only a small minority — currently Andhra Pradesh, Telangana, Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, Maharashtra, and Karnataka (six states). Most states are unicameral, with only a Legislative Assembly.

questions.prompt: The Governor of a State is appointed by:
options: The Chief Justice of the High Court

questions.prompt: What are the qualifications required to be appointed Governor of a State, per Article 157?
options: Citizen of India and at least 25 years of age
Citizen of India and at least 35 years of age
Must have served as a Minister
Must be a member of the State Legislature
questions.explanation: Citizen of India, 35+ years of age.

questions.prompt: Real exam: A member of the Tamil Nadu Cabinet wishing to resign their Ministerial post should submit the resignation letter to:
options: The Speaker of the Legislative Assembly
The President of their political party

questions.prompt: The Governor's power to promulgate Ordinances when the State Legislature is not in session is given under:
Which of the following statements about the Chief Minister's appointment is TRUE?
options: The Chief Minister is appointed by the President
A person who is not a member of the State Legislature can never be appointed Chief Minister
Other Ministers are appointed by the Governor on the advice of the Chief Minister
The Chief Minister is elected directly by the State's voters
questions.explanation: Other Ministers are appointed by the Governor on the CM's advice (Article 164(1)).

questions.prompt: Assertion [A]: The Chief Minister holds office during the pleasure of the Governor. Reason [R]: The Chief Minister is appointed by the Governor. Choose the correct option.
options: Both [A] and [R] are true, and [R] is the correct explanation of [A]
Both [A] and [R] are true, but [R] does not explain [A]

questions.prompt: The Council of Ministers, headed by the Chief Minister, exists to aid and advise the Governor — except when the Governor is required to act in:

options: The Speaker and Deputy Speaker of the Legislative Assembly
The Chairman and Deputy Chairman of the Legislative Council
questions.explanation: Speaker and Deputy Speaker of the Legislative Assembly.

questions.prompt: Which Article gives the Deputy Chairman of the Legislative Council the power to perform the duties of the Chairman's office?
Can one person be appointed Governor of two or more States simultaneously?
Approximately how many Indian states currently have a bicameral legislature (with a Legislative Council)?
questions.explanation: Six (e.g., UP, Bihar, Maharashtra, Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh, Telangana) — most states are unicameral.
